extracted.title: LIFTED: Force Majeure: Mainline-300 (AlexSEG) Effective ID1 Cycle for March 18, 2026
extracted.description:
Columbia Gulf Transmission, LLC (CGT) notifies customers the Force Majeure on Mainline-300 between Delhi Compressor Station and Alexandria Compressor Station is hereby lifted. As a result, the backhaul capacity through AlexSEG has been reinstated to Total Capacity effective Intraday 1 Cycle for Gas Day March 18, 2026.

Please contact your Customer Services Representative with any questions.

***Previous Posting***

Columbia Gulf Transmission, LLC (CGT) notifies customers of the following update regarding the Force Majeure on Mainline-300 between Delhi and Alexandria Compressor Stations in Louisiana.

The work necessitated by the immediate pressure reduction is ongoing. As a reminder, the backhaul capacity through AlexSEG is reduced to 2,325,000 Dth. CGT anticipates this capacity reduction will remain in effect until the Force Majeure is lifted.

At this time, CGT anticipates the Force Majeure will remain in effect through Gas Day Wednesday, March 18, 2026. CGT reminds customers the anticipated timeline and capacity settings are subject to change based on operating conditions during the necessary pressure reduction. CGT will provide updates as additional information becomes available.

CGT encourages customers to monitor the scheduled volumes on the Operationally Available Capacity screen on Informational Postings, as nominations can fluctuate based on market conditions.

Reservation Charge Credits (RCCs) will be determined per the process set forth in the General Terms and Conditions, Section 40 of CGT’s FERC Gas Tariff (Tariff).

Please contact your Customer Services Representative with any questions.

***Previous Posting***

Columbia Gulf Transmission, LLC (CGT) notifies customers of the need for an immediate pressure reduction on Mainline-300 between Delhi Compressor Station and Alexandria Compressor Station in Louisiana. This necessary measure is being taken due to anomalies discovered during a recent in-line inspection of the pipeline pursuant to PHMSA regulation 49 CFR 192.933. Therefore, pursuant to Section 15 of the General Terms and Conditions of CGT’s FERC Gas Tariff, CGT is declaring a Force Majeure effective Gas Day Tuesday, March 10, 2026, and continuing until further notice.

In an effort to minimize the commercial impact, the previously posted planned maintenance at Alexandria Compressor Station Unit 908 (Notice ID: 25982011), scheduled to begin on March 10, 2026, has been rescheduled to a later date yet to be determined.

As a result of the Force Majeure, the Backhaul Capacity through AlexSEG will be reduced to 2,325,000 Dth effective Evening Cycle for Gas Day March 11, 2026.

CGT anticipates this restriction may result in curtailments to Interruptible, Secondary Firm, and Primary Firm services. Please see the details provided below for more information.

AlexSEG
- Capacity Setting: 2,325,000 Dth
- Design Capacity: 2,774,756 Dth
- Posted Percentage: 84%

CGT encourages customers to monitor the scheduled volumes on the Operationally Available Capacity screen on Informational Postings, as nominations can fluctuate based on market conditions.

Reservation Charge Credits (RCCs) will be determined per the process set forth in the General Terms and Conditions, Section 40 of CGT’s FERC Gas Tariff (Tariff). By means of this notice, CGT is establishing the 7-day Average Usage Quantity to be the period of December 30, 2025 – January 5, 2026, pursuant to the provisions in the Tariff.

Please contact your Customer Services Representative with any questions.
